summary: Adds DiffReview walkthrough support: a generated .walkthrough.json can now drive an ordered review inside the DiffReviewStatus buffer via ow. The change adds the local walkthrough skill and schema, implements the runtime resolver/navigation/floating-comment UI, wires it through the status buffer through a narrow host interface, configures editor-side JSON schema validation, and covers the workflow with a headless Neovim integration test plus subsystem notes.

steps.title: Track the Skill
steps.comment: .gitignore: The repository keeps most of codex/ ignored while explicitly allowing checked-in custom skills. The .system skill cache stays ignored so only project-owned skill files participate in review.

steps.title: Walkthrough Skill Contract
steps.comment: codex/skills/walkthrough/SKILL.md: The new skill defines the generation workflow for .walkthrough.json: collect the repo root, HEAD, diff, and untracked files, then write ordered review steps using post-change line positions. This establishes the producer side of the feature consumed by the Neovim plugin.

steps.title: Schema Contract
steps.comment: nvim/lua/diff_review/walkthrough.schema.json: The runtime schema pins the walkthrough file shape to a version, summary, full HEAD commit, and a non-empty step list. Keeping this schema next to the module gives both jsonls and users a local contract for generated walkthrough files.

steps.title: Document Parsing
steps.comment: nvim/lua/diff_review/walkthrough.lua: The module exposes reader injection for tests, reads .walkthrough.json, and tolerantly validates decoded documents into normalized step records. The parser keeps malformed walkthroughs from entering mode while still accepting practical JSON output from the generator.

steps.title: Resolve Steps
steps.comment: nvim/lua/diff_review/walkthrough.lua: resolve_step maps a generated file/line region onto the rendered DiffReviewStatus rows. It groups candidate right-side diff rows by section so exact matches can prefer the most useful current diff section before falling back later in the function.

steps.title: Unfold Hidden Hunks
steps.comment: nvim/lua/diff_review/walkthrough.lua: ensure_expanded opens the section, file, and hunk folds for the current step through host callbacks before retrying resolution. This keeps walkthrough navigation tied to the status buffer's real fold model instead of duplicating rendering state.

steps.title: Comment Floats
steps.comment: nvim/lua/diff_review/walkthrough.lua: Each step opens a non-focusable floating comment near the highlighted diff rows, including stale-position notes and navigation hints. This keeps author intent visible while the reviewer is positioned on the corresponding diff row.

steps.title: Mode Lifecycle
steps.comment: nvim/lua/diff_review/walkthrough.lua: show_step owns cursor movement, row highlighting, expansion retry, and missing-target degradation for an active walkthrough step. This is the core transition from a JSON step to a visible region in DiffReviewStatus.

steps.title: Entry Point
steps.comment: nvim/lua/diff_review/walkthrough.lua: start loads the repo-root .walkthrough.json, decodes it, validates the normalized document, and blocks invalid files before mode state is created. That keeps all user-facing entry checks in one place.

steps.title: Render Reapply
steps.comment: nvim/lua/diff_review/init.lua: Status re-renders now ask an already-loaded walkthrough module to reapply decorations after rows shift. The package.loaded guard keeps the normal render path from paying module load cost until a walkthrough has actually started.

steps.title: Host Seam
steps.comment: nvim/lua/diff_review/init.lua: _walkthrough_host is the narrow interface between the large status implementation and the new walkthrough module. It hands over only cwd, state access, fold keys, rerendering, and async git lookup instead of letting walkthrough reach into init internals directly.

steps.title: Status Command
steps.comment: nvim/lua/diff_review/init.lua: The status command table gains a walkthrough command so the configured key participates in the same command metadata path as push, pull, PR, and browse. The actual keymap then starts the new module through the host seam.

steps.title: Default Key
steps.comment: nvim/lua/diff_review/config.lua: The public keymap config adds a walkthrough action with ow as the default binding. This makes the feature discoverable through the existing DiffReview keymap configuration instead of hard-coding the key in the runtime.

steps.title: JSON LSP Wiring
steps.comment: nvim/lua/plugins/lsp.lua: jsonls now validates .walkthrough.json against the local DiffReview schema. That gives immediate editor feedback when a generated walkthrough is malformed or points at an invalid shape.

steps.title: Integration Coverage
steps.comment: nvim/tests/diff_review/walkthrough.lua: The headless integration test starts with the happy path: open status, launch ow, accept the summary, expand the relevant diff rows, move the cursor to the generated line, and show the comment float. This proves the user-visible workflow works through the real status renderer.
